name: Sequence-to-Sequence
oneliner: An encoder-decoder architecture that reads an input sequence in full, then generates an output sequence token by token.
explanation: Sequence-to-sequence models use an encoder to process the full input with bidirectional attention, producing context vectors, and a decoder to generate the output autoregressively using causal self-attention plus cross-attention over the encoder's output. This design is natural for tasks where input and output are different sequences: translation, summarization, and question answering. T5 and BART are the most prominent seq2seq transformers. While decoder-only models like GPT dominate chat applications, seq2seq models remain strong for structured generation tasks and are the architecture behind most neural machine translation systems.
fundamentals: The encoder produces $H_{\text{enc}} = \text{Encoder}(x_1, \ldots, x_n)$ using bidirectional attention. The decoder at step $t$ computes causal self-attention over $y_{<t}$, then cross-attention: $\text{softmax}\left(\frac{Q_{\text{dec}} K_{\text{enc}}^\top}{\sqrt{d_k}}\right) V_{\text{enc}}$. Encoder KV cache computed once during prefill.

foundational_papers.title: Exploring the Limits of Transfer Learning with a Unified Text-to-Text Transformer
